4340	  Let \(S \subseteq R\) be a submonoid of a commutative ring and let
4341	  \(M_0 \xrightarrow{\,a\,} M_1 \xrightarrow{\,b\,} M_2\) be \(R\)-linear maps forming an exact
4342	  sequence at \(M_1\) (the image of \(a\) equals the kernel of \(b\)). Fix, for each \(i\), a
4343	  localisation map \(\ell_i : M_i \to M_i'\) at \(S\) (so each \(M_i'\) is the localised module
4344	  \(S^{-1} M_i\)). Then the induced maps \(S^{-1} a : M_0' \to M_1'\) and
4345	  \(S^{-1} b : M_1' \to M_2'\) again form an exact sequence at \(M_1'\). In other words,
4346	  localisation at \(S\) is an exact functor; in particular it carries short exact sequences to short
4347	  exact sequences and commutes
4348	  with the formation of kernels.
4349	\end{lemma}
4350	
4351	\begin{lemma}[Base-ring descent of a localisation along an algebra map]
4352	  \label{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap}
4353	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap}
4354	  Let \(A\) be an \(R\)-algebra, let \(M\) and \(N\) be \(A\)-modules that are also \(R\)-modules
4355	  compatibly with the algebra structure (\(R \to A \to M\) and \(R \to A \to N\) are scalar towers),
4356	  and let \(\varphi : M \to N\) be \(A\)-linear. Fix \(f \in R\) and write \(f_A\) for its image in
4357	  \(A\) under the algebra structure map \(R \to A\). If \(\varphi\) exhibits \(N\) as the localisation of
4358	  \(M\) at the powers of \(f_A\) (as \(A\)-modules), then
4359	  \(\varphi\), viewed as an \(R\)-linear map via restriction of scalars along \(R \to A\), exhibits
4360	  \(N\) as the localisation of \(M\) at the powers of \(f\) (as \(R\)-modules).
4361	\end{lemma}
4362	\begin{proof}
4363	  % NOTE (Lean): converse of IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars; unit clause uses Module.End.isUnit_iff.
4364	  This is the converse of the scalar-restriction direction of the localised-module API (which
4365	  descends a localisation at a submonoid \(S \subseteq R\) to its image in \(A\)); Mathlib does not
4366	  provide this direction, so it is recorded here as a project lemma. It is elementary. The three
4367	  defining clauses of being a localised module transfer directly. Surjectivity-of-quotients
4368	  and injectivity-up-to-torsion are statements about the underlying additive maps and are unchanged by
4369	  restricting scalars. For the unit clause one must see that multiplication by \(f^k\) on \(N\) is
4370	  bijective as an \(R\)-endomorphism: by the scalar tower \(f^k \cdot x = f_A^{\,k} \cdot x\) for every
4371	  \(x\), the \(R\)-endomorphism \(\,x \mapsto f^k \cdot x\,\) coincides as a function with the
4372	  \(A\)-endomorphism \(\,x \mapsto f_A^{\,k} \cdot x\,\), and bijectivity is a property of the
4373	  underlying map independent of the choice of base ring. Hence all three clauses hold over \(R\).
4374	\end{proof}
4375	
4376	\begin{lemma}[Image opens of the affine tile identification]
4377	  \label{lem:tile_image_opens_identities}
4378	  % NOTE: the \lean{} pin is deferred until the Lean decl is built; sync will reconcile it.
4379	  \uses{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen}
4380	  % NOTE (Lean): in the structure-sheaf restriction calculus these equalities read
4381	  %   (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ ⊤) = specBasicOpen g  and
4382	  %   (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ D(f̄)) = specBasicOpen(gf).
4383	  Let \(g, f \in R\), let \(R_g = R[g^{-1}]\) be the localisation of \(R\) away from \(g\), and
4384	  let \(\iota : \Spec R_g \xrightarrow{\sim} D(g) \hookrightarrow X\) be the open immersion
4385	  identifying \(\Spec R_g\) with the basic open \(D(g) \subseteq X = \Spec R\) (the composite of the
4386	  canonical homeomorphism \(\Spec R_g \xrightarrow{\sim} D(g)\) with the open inclusion). Then the image
4387	  opens of \(\iota\) of the two relevant opens of \(\Spec R_g\) are
4388	  \[
4389	    \iota\bigl(\Spec R_g\bigr) = D(g),
4390	    \qquad
4391	    \iota\bigl(D(\bar f)\bigr) = D(gf) = D(g) \cap D(f),
4392	  \]
4393	  where \(\bar f\) is the image of \(f\) under the localisation map \(R \to R_g\).
4394	\end{lemma}
4395	\begin{proof}
4396	  \uses{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen}
4397	  The canonical homeomorphism \(\Spec R_g \xrightarrow{\sim} D(g)\) carries \(\Spec R_g\)
4398	  isomorphically onto \(D(g)\), so the first identity is just the image of the whole space under that
4399	  homeomorphism followed by the open inclusion. For the second, \(\bar f\) is the image of \(f\) under
4400	  \(R \to R_g\); its basic open \(D(\bar f) \subseteq \Spec R_g\) maps under the homeomorphism to
4401	  \(D(g) \cap D(f)\), since restricting to \(D(g)\) and then taking the locus where \(f\) is invertible
4402	  is the locus where both \(g\) and \(f\) are invertible. Finally \(D(g) \cap D(f) = D(gf)\) since the
4403	  basic open of a product is the intersection of the basic opens. These are equalities of opens, proved
4404	  set-theoretically; they are not definitional.
4405	\end{proof}
4406	
4407	\begin{lemma}[Natural section comparison between the tile and the ambient sheaf]
4408	  \label{lem:tile_section_comparison}
4409	  % NOTE: the \lean{} pin is deferred until the Lean decl is built; sync will reconcile it.
4410	  \uses{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib,
4411	    lem:tile_image_opens_identities}
4412	  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities}, with
4413	  \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\) the restriction of the quasi-coherent module
4414	  \(\mathcal{F}\) along \(\iota : \Spec R_g \cong D(g) \hookrightarrow X\), there is an \(R_g\)-linear
4415	  isomorphism, natural in the open \(V \subseteq \Spec R_g\) and intertwining the restriction maps of the
4416	  two sheaves,
4417	  \[
4418	    \Gamma_{R_g}\bigl(V, \mathcal{F}_{(g)}\bigr) \;\cong\; \Gamma_R\bigl(\iota(V), \mathcal{F}\bigr).
4419	  \]
4420	  % NOTE (Lean): Γ_{R_g}(−, F_(g)) is modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (ModuleCat R_g-valued);
4421	  % Γ_R(−, F) the corresponding functor for F over R.
4422	  Here \(\Gamma_{R_g}(-, \mathcal{F}_{(g)})\) is the global-ring section functor attached to the
4423	  presented tile over \(R_g\) (valued in \(R_g\)-modules), and \(\Gamma_R(-, \mathcal{F})\) the
4424	  corresponding functor for \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(R\).
4425	\end{lemma}
4426	\begin{proof}
4427	  \uses{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib,
4428	    lem:tile_image_opens_identities}
4429	  % NOTE (Lean): this is the load-bearing ~100--150 LOC construction.
4430	  % The local-ring functor is restrict_obj/restrict_map (defeq, Lemma lem:restrict_obj_mathlib).
4431	  % The global-ring functor is modulesSpecToSheaf.obj = forgetToSheafModuleCat composed with
4432	  % restrict-scalars along globalSectionsIso. The bridge is NOT rfl: the two section objects
4433	  % (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F_(g)).presheaf.obj V and (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (ι V)
4434	  % differ by both the base ring and the provably-equal-but-not-rfl opens.
4435	  At the level of the local-ring section functor --- the sheaf-of-modules functor
4436	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal{M},-)\) valued over the stalk ring \(\Gamma(V,\mathcal{O})\) --- the equality
4437	  \(\Gamma(\mathcal{F}_{(g)},V) = \Gamma(\mathcal{F},\iota(V))\) holds definitionally
4438	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib}). The content of the present lemma is to upgrade this
4439	  definitional equality from the local-ring functor \(\Gamma(\mathcal{M},-)\) to the global-ring
4440	  section functor \(\Gamma_{R_g}(-,\mathcal{F}_{(g)})\) (resp.\ \(\Gamma_R(-,\mathcal{F})\)). The
4441	  global-ring functor is obtained from the local-ring one by composing with restriction of scalars along
4442	  the global-sections identification \(R_g \cong \Gamma(\top,\mathcal{O}_{\Spec R_g})\)
4443	  (resp.\ \(R \cong \Gamma(\top,\mathcal{O}_X)\)). Concretely: take the definitional identification of
4444	  underlying additive groups, transport it across both global-sections restriction-of-scalars bookkeeping
4445	  maps to obtain an \(R_g\)-linear isomorphism, and verify naturality in \(V\) and compatibility with
4446	  restriction maps. The image opens that appear are identified by
4447	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities}. This bridge is genuinely non-definitional: the two
4448	  global-ring section modules \(\Gamma_{R_g}(V,\mathcal{F}_{(g)})\) and
4449	  \(\Gamma_R(\iota(V),\mathcal{F})\) are not definitionally equal --- they differ both by the base
4450	  ring (\(R_g\) versus \(R\)) and by the provably-equal but not-definitionally-equal opens of
4451	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities}.
4452	\end{proof}
4453	
4454	\begin{lemma}[Per-tile section localisation at \(f\)]
4455	  \label{lem:tile_section_localization}
4456	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tile_section_localization}
4457	  \uses{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover, lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen,
4458	    lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation, lem:restrict_obj_mathlib,
4459	    lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap,
4460	    lem:tile_image_opens_identities, lem:tile_section_comparison}
4461	  Let \(X = \Spec R\), let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, let \(f \in R\),
4462	  and let \(g \in R\) be either a cover element \(g = g_j\) or an overlap product \(g = g_j g_k\) of the
4463	  finite presentation cover of Lemma~\ref{lem:qcoh_finite_presentation_cover}, so that the restriction \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\) of
4464	  \(\mathcal{F}\) to \(\Spec R_g\) admits a global presentation. Then the restriction-of-sections map
4465	  \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\) exhibits its target as the localisation of
4466	  its source at the powers of \(f\); equivalently, there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
4467	  \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\).
4468	\end{lemma}
4469	\begin{proof}
4470	  \uses{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen, lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation,
4471	    lem:restrict_obj_mathlib, lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap,
4472	    lem:tile_image_opens_identities, lem:tile_section_comparison}
4473	  % NOTE: the naive recipe "the section comparison is the restrict_obj rfl" is UNSOUND:
4474	  % restrict_obj is rfl only for the local-ring SheafOfModules functor Γ(M,-), whereas
4475	  % the localisation in this file lives in the global-ring functor (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj),
4476	  % which does NOT commute with restriction definitionally. Hence the five-step descent below.
4477	  Let \(\iota : \Spec R_g \xrightarrow{\sim} D(g) \hookrightarrow X\) be the open immersion of
4478	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities}, with \(\bar f\) the image of \(f\) under the
4479	  localisation map \(R \to R_g\). The proof is a base-ring descent in five steps; the section
4480	  comparison it rests on is \emph{not} the definitional equality of
4481	  Lemma~\ref{lem:restrict_obj_mathlib} (see the proof of
4482	  Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_section_comparison}).
4483	
4484	  \emph{Step 1 (global presentation of the tile).} Since \(D(g)\) lies in a presentation member of the
4485	  cover, step B4 (Lemma~\ref{lem:presentation_modulesRestrictBasicOpen}) presents the tile
4486	  \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\) globally over \(\Spec R_g\).
4487	
4488	  \emph{Step 2 (localisation over \(R_g\)).} Apply
4489	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation} to the globally presented
4490	  \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\) and \(\bar f\): the section-restriction map
4491	  \(\Gamma_{R_g}(\Spec R_g, \mathcal{F}_{(g)}) \to \Gamma_{R_g}(D(\bar f), \mathcal{F}_{(g)})\)
4492	  exhibits its target as a localisation at the powers of \(\bar f\), \emph{as \(R_g\)-modules}. Note this
4493	  is over \(R_g\), not yet over \(R\).
4494	
4495	  \emph{Step 3 (opens identities).} By Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_image_opens_identities} the two image opens of
4496	  \(\iota\) are \(\iota(\Spec R_g) = D(g)\) and \(\iota(D(\bar f)) = D(gf) = D(g) \cap D(f)\).
4497	
4498	  \emph{Step 4 (the section comparison).} By Lemma~\ref{lem:tile_section_comparison} there is an
4499	  \(R_g\)-linear isomorphism, natural in \(V\) and intertwining restriction maps,
4500	  \(\Gamma_{R_g}(V, \mathcal{F}_{(g)}) \cong \Gamma_R(\iota(V), \mathcal{F})\). This is the load-bearing
4501	  step: it bridges the global-ring section functor (where Step 2's localisation lives) and the ambient
4502	  sheaf \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(R\); it is a genuine natural-isomorphism construction, not a definitional
4503	  identification. Transporting Step 2's localisation-at-\(\bar f\) property across this isomorphism
4504	  (\(R_g\)-linearly) and matching source and target opens by Step 3 yields: the map
4505	  \(\Gamma_R(D(g), \mathcal{F}) \to \Gamma_R(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\) exhibits its target as a
4506	  localisation at the powers of \(\bar f\), \emph{still as \(R_g\)-modules}.
4507	
4508	  \emph{Step 5 (base-ring descent to \(R\)).} Finally descend the base ring from \(R_g\) to \(R\) by
4509	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap}, applied with \(A = R_g\) and the
4510	  scalar tower \(R \to R_g\) on the two section modules: since \(\bar f\) is the image of \(f\) under
4511	  \(R \to R_g\), the powers of \(\bar f\) descend to the powers of \(f\), and the \(R\)-linear
4512	  restriction of the Step-4 map exhibits \(\Gamma_R(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\) as the localisation of
4513	  \(\Gamma_R(D(g), \mathcal{F})\) at the powers of \(f\) \emph{over \(R\)}. Equivalently there is a
4514	  canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism \(\Gamma(D(g), \mathcal{F})_f \cong \Gamma(D(gf), \mathcal{F})\).
4515	
4516	  Crucially this localisation is carried out entirely on the tile \(\mathcal{F}_{(g)}\), where
4517	  \(\mathcal{F}\) is associated to a module (the tile is globally presented, hence of the form
4518	  \(\widetilde{M_g}\)), and never on
4519	  the global object \(\mathcal{F}\). This is exactly the structural feature that keeps the keystone

16	/-!
17	# Restriction of an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module to a basic open (Stacks 01I8) — Route-P step P1a
18	
19	Project-local supplement for the affine quasi-coherence equivalence.

24	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

26	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — continuity of `Opens.overEquivalence`
27	
28	Mathlib provides `TopologicalSpace.Opens.overEquivalence U : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U` but, as recorded in
29	its source `## TODO`, does *not* yet prove that the two functors are continuous for the relevant
30	Grothendieck topologies.  These instances supply exactly that (the gateway brick for the Route B
31	restrict–over bridge B3): both functors are cover-preserving, and `CompatiblePreserving` is automatic
32	for the cover-dense functors of an equivalence, so `Functor.IsContinuous` follows. -/
33	
34	open TopologicalSpace in
35	/-- The forward functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` preserves covers. -/
36	theorem Opens.overEquivalence_functor_coverPreserving
37	    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
38	    CoverPreserving ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U)
39	      (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor where
40	  cover_preserve {Y S} hS := by
41	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff] at hS
42	    intro y hy
43	    obtain ⟨V, f, hVf, hyV⟩ := hS y.1 hy
44	    obtain ⟨W, h, h', hSh, hfeq⟩ := hVf
45	    refine ⟨(Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.obj W,
46	      (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.map h, ⟨W, h, 𝟙 _, hSh, by simp⟩, ?_⟩
47	    show y.1 ∈ W.left
48	    exact leOfHom h' hyV
49	
50	open TopologicalSpace in
51	/-- The inverse functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` preserves covers. -/
52	theorem Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_coverPreserving
53	    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
54	    CoverPreserving (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U)
55	      (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse where
56	  cover_preserve {Y S} hS := by
57	    rw [GrothendieckTopology.mem_over_iff]
58	    intro x hx
59	    obtain ⟨⟨x', hx'U⟩, hx'Y, rfl⟩ := hx
60	    obtain ⟨P, f, hSf, hxP⟩ := hS ⟨x', hx'U⟩ hx'Y
61	    exact ⟨((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj P).left,
62	      ((Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map f).left,
63	      ⟨(Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.obj P, (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.map f, 𝟙 _,
64	        ⟨P, f, 𝟙 _, hSf, by simp⟩, by simp⟩,
65	      ⟨⟨x', hx'U⟩, hxP, rfl⟩⟩
66	
67	open TopologicalSpace in
68	/-- The forward functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. -/
69	instance Opens.overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous
70	    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
71	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).functor.IsContinuous
72	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) :=
73	  Functor.IsCoverDense.isContinuous _ _ _ (Opens.overEquivalence_functor_coverPreserving U)
74	
75	open TopologicalSpace in
76	/-- The inverse functor of `Opens.overEquivalence U` is continuous. -/
77	instance Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous
78	    {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (U : Opens X) :
79	    (Opens.overEquivalence U).inverse.IsContinuous
80	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥U) ((Opens.grothendieckTopology X).over U) :=
81	  Functor.IsCoverDense.isContinuous _ _ _ (Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_coverPreserving U)

83	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — restriction of modules to a basic open -/
84	
85	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R)
86	
87	/-- The basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec R`, as an open subscheme of `Spec R`. -/
88	abbrev specBasicOpen : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f
89	
90	/-- The localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R` factoring through `D(f)`: the inverse of the
91	affine identification `D(f) ≅ Spec R_f` followed by the open immersion of `D(f)`. -/
92	noncomputable abbrev specAwayToSpec :
93	    Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f)) ⟶ Spec R :=
94	  (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv ≫ (specBasicOpen f).ι
95	
96	/-- **Stacks 01I8, `lemma-widetilde-pullback`.** Restriction of an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module to the basic
97	open `D(f)`, transported to a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R_f}`-modules along the affine identification
98	`D(f) ≅ Spec R_f`.  Built by restricting first along the open immersion `D(f) ↪ Spec R`, then along
99	the (iso, hence open) inverse of `basicOpenIsoSpecAway f`.  Project-local: assembles two Mathlib
100	restrictions into the single transport used by the affine quasi-coherence equivalence. -/
101	noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpen (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
102	    (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f))).Modules :=
103	  (F.restrict (specBasicOpen f).ι).restrict (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv
104	
105	/-- **Stacks 01I8, `lemma-widetilde-pullback`.** The transported restriction
106	`modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F` is canonically isomorphic to the inverse image of `F` along the
107	localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R`.  This identifies the double restriction with a single
108	pullback, and is the comparison isomorphism `F|_{D(f)} ≅ F_{(f)}` of the blueprint.  Project-local:
109	reconciles the (good-defeq) iterated `restrict` with the conceptual inverse-image description. -/
110	noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpenIso (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
111	    modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F ≅
112	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (specAwayToSpec f)).obj F :=
113	  ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorComp (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv (specBasicOpen f).ι).app F).symm
114	    ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (specAwayToSpec f)).app F
115	
116	/-- The localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R` of `specAwayToSpec` is exactly the spectrum of the
117	away-localisation ring map `R → R_f`.  Project-local: identifies the geometric transport map with
118	the algebraic `Spec.map (algebraMap …)`, so that pullback along it computes base change. -/
119	theorem specAwayToSpec_eq :
120	    specAwayToSpec f = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away f))) := by
121	  rw [specAwayToSpec, Iso.inv_comp_eq]
122	  exact (IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac _ _ _).symm

124	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B presentation transport (B2–B4) -/
125	
126	open SheafOfModules in
127	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
128	/-- **Route B, step B2.** If `M.over U` carries a presentation and `D(g) ⊆ U`, then the further
129	over-restriction `M.over D(g)` admits a presentation. -/
130	noncomputable def presentationOverBasicOpen
131	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens)
132	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) (g : R) (hg : specBasicOpen g ≤ U) :
133	    (M.over (specBasicOpen g)).Presentation :=
134	  letI W : Over U := Over.mk (homOfLE hg)
135	  letI e : SheafOfModules.{u} ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over W.left) ≌
136	      SheafOfModules.{u} (((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U).over W) :=
137	    pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence
138	    (Over.iteratedSliceEquiv W)
139	    (S := ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U).over W)
140	    (R := (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over W.left) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
141	    (by ext : 2; exact (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.1.map_id _)
142	    (by ext : 2; exact (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.1.map_id _)
143	  letI P1 : ((M.over U).over W).Presentation :=
144	    P.map (pushforward (𝟙 (((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over U).over W))) (by rfl)
145	  letI P2 : (e.inverse.obj ((M.over U).over W)).Presentation :=
146	    P1.map e.inverse (.refl _)
147	  letI iso : e.inverse.obj ((M.over U).over W) ≅ M.over W.left :=
148	    e.fullyFaithfulFunctor.preimageIso
149	      (by exact e.counitIso.app ((M.over U).over W))
150	  show (M.over W.left).Presentation from Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} iso.hom P2
151

156	private lemma specBasicOpen_ι_image_overEquivalence_functor (g : R) (V : Over (specBasicOpen g)) :
157	    (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor.obj V = V.left := by
158	  apply Opens.ext
159	  exact Set.image_preimage_eq_of_subset (fun x hx => ⟨⟨x, leOfHom V.hom hx⟩, rfl⟩)

161	/-- Continuity of `overEquivalence.functor` phrased for the open-subscheme carrier (defeq to the
162	plain subtype, but instance search needs the `toScheme` form to fire). -/
163	instance overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous_toScheme (g : R) :
164	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor.IsContinuous
165	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))
166	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme) :=
167	  Opens.overEquivalence_functor_isContinuous (specBasicOpen g)

169	/-- Continuity of `overEquivalence.inverse` phrased for the open-subscheme carrier. -/
170	instance overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous_toScheme (g : R) :
171	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse.IsContinuous
172	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)
173	      ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g)) :=
174	  Opens.overEquivalence_inverse_isContinuous (specBasicOpen g)

176	/-- The forgetful functor `Over D(g) ⥤ Opens (Spec R)` agrees (via the open-immersion image–preimage
177	identity `ι ''ᵁ (ι ⁻¹ᵁ V) = V` for `V ≤ D(g)`) with the over-site equivalence followed by the
178	open-immersion `opensFunctor`. In the thin `Opens` category naturality is automatic. Project-local:
179	the geometric datum underlying the Route B restrict–over bridge (B3a). -/
180	noncomputable def overForgetIso (g : R) :
181	    Over.forget (specBasicOpen g) ≅
182	      (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor ⋙ (specBasicOpen g).ι.opensFunctor :=
183	  NatIso.ofComponents
184	    (fun V => eqToIso (specBasicOpen_ι_image_overEquivalence_functor g V).symm)
185	    (fun {_ _} _ => Subsingleton.elim _ _)
186	
187	/-- The structure-sheaf comparison `φ` feeding `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence`: the over-picture
188	ring sheaf `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over D(g)` maps to the `overEquivalence`-pushforward of the
189	subscheme ring sheaf. Built by whiskering `overForgetIso.inv` into `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf`. -/
190	noncomputable def overBasicOpenRingHom (g : R) :
191	    Sheaf.over (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (specBasicOpen g) ⟶
192	      ((Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).functor.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
193	        ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))
194	        (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)).obj
195	      (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.ringCatSheaf :=
196	  ⟨Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op (overForgetIso g).inv) (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val⟩
197	
198	/-- The inverse over-site equivalence followed by `Over.forget` is *definitionally* the
199	open-immersion `opensFunctor` (`overEquivalence.inverse` sends `W` to `⟨Subtype.val '' W, _⟩`, whose
200	`.left` is `ι ''ᵁ W`). Project-local: the (trivial) reverse datum of the B3 bridge. -/
201	noncomputable def overForgetInvIso (g : R) :
202	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse ⋙ Over.forget (specBasicOpen g) ≅
203	      (specBasicOpen g).ι.opensFunctor :=
204	  Iso.refl _
205	
206	/-- The reverse structure-sheaf comparison `ψ` feeding `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` (whiskering
207	of `overForgetInvIso.inv`, which is the identity). -/
208	noncomputable def overBasicOpenRingInvHom (g : R) :
209	    (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.ringCatSheaf ⟶
210	      ((Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse.sheafPushforwardContinuous RingCat
211	        (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(specBasicOpen g).toScheme)
212	        ((Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R)).over (specBasicOpen g))).obj
213	      (Sheaf.over (Spec R).ringCatSheaf (specBasicOpen g)) :=
214	  ⟨Functor.whiskerRight (NatTrans.op (overForgetInvIso g).inv) (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val⟩
215	
216	/-- **Route B, step B3 (the load-bearing bridge — engine).** The equivalence of categories of sheaves
217	of modules between the open subscheme `D(g)` and the over-site `(Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over D(g)`,
218	obtained from `pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence` along the (continuous) over-site equivalence
219	`Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)` fed the structure-sheaf comparison data
220	`overBasicOpenRingHom`/`overBasicOpenRingInvHom`. Its functor sends a subscheme module
221	`F.restrict ι` to the over-picture restriction `F.over D(g)` (agreeing on sections by `restrict_obj`);
222	this object correspondence is the bridge `overBasicOpenIsoRestrict` consumed by B4. -/
223	noncomputable def modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence (g : R) :
224	    (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.Modules ≌
225	      SheafOfModules.{u} ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.over (specBasicOpen g)) :=
226	  pushforwardPushforwardEquivalence (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g))
227	    (overBasicOpenRingHom g) (overBasicOpenRingInvHom g)
228	    (by
229	      refine NatTrans.ext (funext fun (V : (Opens ↥(specBasicOpen g))ᵒᵖ) => ?_)
230	      simp only [overBasicOpenRingHom, overBasicOpenRingInvHom, NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app,
231	        Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj]
232	      erw [← Functor.map_comp]
233	      exact congrArg (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val.map (Subsingleton.elim _ _))
234	    (by
235	      refine NatTrans.ext (funext fun (V : (Over (specBasicOpen g))ᵒᵖ) => ?_)
236	      simp only [overBasicOpenRingHom, overBasicOpenRingInvHom, NatTrans.comp_app, Functor.whiskerRight_app, NatTrans.op_app,
237	        Functor.whiskerLeft_app, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj, NatTrans.id_app,
238	        overForgetInvIso, Iso.refl_inv]
239	      erw [← Functor.map_comp]
240	      exact (congrArg (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val.map (Subsingleton.elim _ (𝟙 _))).trans
241	        ((Spec R).ringCatSheaf.val.map_id _))

244	/-- **Route B, step B3 object iso.** The bridge: the inverse engine applied to the over-picture
245	restriction `M.over D(g)` is the honest subscheme restriction `M.restrict ι`. -/
246	noncomputable def overBasicOpenIsoRestrict (g : R) (M : (Spec R).Modules) :
247	    (modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse.obj (M.over (specBasicOpen g)) ≅
248	      M.restrict (specBasicOpen g).ι := by

251	    (Opens.overEquivalence (specBasicOpen g)).inverse (Over.forget (specBasicOpen g))

254	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology ↥(Spec R))

257	    (overBasicOpenRingInvHom g)

261	  simp [overBasicOpenRingInvHom, overForgetInvIso, Scheme.Opens.ι_appIso]

266	instance pullbackObjUnitToUnit_isIso_basicOpen (g : R) :

268	      ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).toRingCatSheafHom) := by
269	  haveI : IsIso (((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).base) := inferInstance

277	/-- The restriction-along-the-affine-identification functor sends the structure-sheaf unit to the
278	structure-sheaf unit: `(restrict (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).obj 𝟙_{D(g)} ≅ 𝟙_{Spec R_g}`. Built
279	from `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an iso since `(basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv`

283	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv).obj
284	        (SheafOfModules.unit (specBasicOpen g).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) ≅
285	      SheafOfModules.unit (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away g))).ringCatSheaf :=

291	-- `synthInstance.maxHeartbeats` is insufficient.
292	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 400000 in
293	open SheafOfModules in
294	/-- **Route B, step B4.** If `M.over U` carries a presentation and `D(g) ⊆ U`, then the affine
295	restriction `modulesRestrictBasicOpen g M`, as a `(Spec R_g).Modules`-object, admits a global
296	presentation. Assembled by transporting the B2 over-presentation across the B3 bridge isomorphism
297	`overBasicOpenIsoRestrict` and the restriction along the affine identification. -/
298	noncomputable def presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen
299	    (M : (Spec R).Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens)
300	    (P : (M.over U).Presentation) (g : R) (hg : specBasicOpen g ≤ U) :
301	    (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g M).Presentation := by
302	  letI P2 : (M.over (specBasicOpen g)).Presentation := presentationOverBasicOpen M U P g hg
303	  letI P3 : ((modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse.obj
304	      (M.over (specBasicOpen g))).Presentation :=
305	    P2.map (modulesOverBasicOpenEquivalence g).inverse (.refl _)
306	  letI P4 : (M.restrict (specBasicOpen g).ι).Presentation :=
307	    Presentation.ofIsIso.{u, u, u} (overBasicOpenIsoRestrict g M).hom P3
308	  -- The instance is supplied as `hpc` (and the functor universe pinned to `u`): inline instance
309	  -- search for `PreservesColimitsOfSize` of the affine restriction does not fire on its own here.
310	  haveI hpc : Limits.PreservesColimitsOfSize.{u, u, u, u, u + 1, u + 1}
311	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv) := inferInstance
312	  exact @SheafOfModules.Presentation.map _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ P4
313	    (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor.{u} (basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv) hpc
314	    (restrictBasicOpenUnitIso g)

316	end RestrictOverBridge

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Restrict

open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry TopologicalSpace

universe u
variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

abbrev specBasicOpen (f : R) : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f

-- Sub-lemma A, identity 1: image of ⊤ is D(g)
example (g : R) :
    (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens))
      = specBasicOpen g := by
  sorry

-- Sub-lemma A, identity 2: image of D(f̄) is D(g*f)
example (g f : R) :
    (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f)))
      = specBasicOpen (g * f) := by
  sorry

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Restrict

open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry TopologicalSpace

universe u
variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

abbrev specBasicOpen (f : R) : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f

noncomputable abbrev specAwayToSpec (f : R) :
    Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f)) ⟶ Spec R :=
  (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv ≫ (specBasicOpen f).ι

-- combine two image functors into one
example (g f : R) :
    (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f)))
      = specAwayToSpec g ''ᵁ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f)) := by
  rw [specAwayToSpec, Scheme.Hom.image_comp]

-- now identify specAwayToSpec g with Spec.map algebraMap
example (g : R) : specAwayToSpec g = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))) := by
  rw [specAwayToSpec, Iso.inv_comp_eq]
  exact (IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac _ _ _).symm

===
  spectra, with range consisting of prime ideals disjoint from the submonoid.

* `PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range`: for localization away from an element, the
  range of the embedding is the `basicOpen` associated to the element.

* `PrimeSpectrum.comap_isEmbedding_of_surjective`: a surjective ring homomorphism between
  commutative semirings induces an embedding between the prime spectra.

* `PrimeSpectrum.isClosedEmbedding_comap_of_surjective`: a surjective ring homomorphism between
  commutative rings induces a closed embedding between the prime spectra.

* `PrimeSpectrum.primeSpectrumProdHomeo`: the prime spectrum of a product semiring is homeomorphic
  to the disjoint union of the prime spectra.

* `PrimeSpectrum.stableUnderSpecialization_range_iff`: the range of `PrimeSpectrum.comap _` is
  closed iff it is stable under specialization.

600	theorem localization_away_comap_range (S : Type v) [CommSemiring S] [Algebra R S] (r : R)
601	    [IsLocalization.Away r S] : Set.range (comap (algebraMap R S)) = basicOpen r := by
602	  rw [localization_comap_range S (Submonoid.powers r)]
603	  ext x
604	  simp only [mem_zeroLocus, basicOpen_eq_zeroLocus_compl, SetLike.mem_coe, Set.mem_setOf_eq,
605	    Set.singleton_subset_iff, Set.mem_compl_iff, disjoint_iff_inf_le]
606	  constructor
607	  · intro h₁ h₂
608	    exact h₁ ⟨Submonoid.mem_powers r, h₂⟩
609	  · rintro h₁ _ ⟨⟨n, rfl⟩, h₃⟩
610	    exact h₁ (x.2.mem_of_pow_mem _ h₃)
611	
612	theorem localization_away_isOpenEmbedding (S : Type v) [CommSemiring S] [Algebra R S] (r : R)
613	    [IsLocalization.Away r S] : IsOpenEmbedding (comap (algebraMap R S)) where
614	  toIsEmbedding := localization_comap_isEmbedding S (Submonoid.powers r)
615	  isOpen_range := by
616	    rw [localization_away_comap_range S r]
617	    exact isOpen_basicOpen
618	
619	theorem isCompact_basicOpen (f : R) : IsCompact (basicOpen f : Set (PrimeSpectrum R)) := by
620	  rw [← localization_away_comap_range (Localization (Submonoid.powers f))]
621	  exact isCompact_range (continuous_comap _)
622

624	    TopologicalSpace.Opens.comap ⟨comap f, continuous_comap f⟩ (basicOpen x) = basicOpen (f x) :=
625	  rfl

  rw [Scheme.Hom.image, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul]

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde
import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Restrict
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry TopologicalSpace
universe u
variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
abbrev specBasicOpen (f : R) : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f

example (g f : R) :
    (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)))) ''ᵁ
        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f))
      = specBasicOpen (g * f) := by
  apply Opens.ext
  rw [Scheme.Hom.coe_image]
  rw [show ⇑(Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))))
      = PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)) from rfl]
  ext x
  simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, Opens.coe_inf, Set.mem_inter_iff,
    Set.mem_image, PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen]
  constructor
  · rintro ⟨p, hp, rfl⟩
    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
    · -- g ∉ comap p  : algebraMap g is a unit in Localization.Away g, hence not in prime p
      show (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)) g ∉ p.asIdeal
      intro hmem
      exact p.asIdeal.isPrime.ne_top (Ideal.eq_top_of_isUnit_mem _ hmem
        (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g))
    · show (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)) f ∉ p.asIdeal; exact hp
  · rintro ⟨hg, hf⟩
    -- x ∈ basicOpen g = range comap
    have hx : x ∈ Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))) := by
      rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range (Localization.Away g) g]
      exact hg
    obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := hx
    exact ⟨p, hf, rfl⟩

example (g f : R) :
    (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)))) ''ᵁ
        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f))
      = specBasicOpen (g * f) := by

    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
    · show (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)) g ∉ p.asIdeal

    obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := hx
    exact ⟨p, hf, rfl⟩

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Route B keystone: per-tile section localization

`tile_section_localization` (Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8, the last keystone leaf) shows that for a
quasi-coherent `F` on `Spec R` and elements `f g : R` with the tile `F_{(g)}` globally presented, the
section-restriction `Γ(D(g), F) → Γ(D(gf), F)` exhibits its target as the localization of its source
at the powers of `f`.  It is the per-tile localization datum the sheaf-axiom kernel comparison
consumes (`analogies/keystone-descent.md`).

The naive recipe "the section comparison is the `restrict_obj` rfl" is UNSOUND: `restrict_obj` is rfl
only for the local-ring `SheafOfModules` section functor `Γ(M,-)`, whereas the localization lives in
the global-ring functor `modulesSpecToSheaf.obj`, which does NOT commute with restriction
definitionally.  Hence the honest base-ring descent: Sub-lemma A (opens identities,
`tile_image_opens_identities`) + Sub-lemma B (the load-bearing natural section comparison,
`tile_section_comparison`) + the DONE base-ring descent
`isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap`. -/

section TileSectionLocalization

open TopologicalSpace

/-- **Sub-lemma A (Stacks 01I8): image opens of the affine tile identification.**  Let `g f : R`, let
`R_g = Localization.Away g`, and let `ι = specAwayToSpec g : Spec R_g ⟶ Spec R` be the localization
morphism identifying `Spec R_g` with `D(g) ⊆ Spec R`.  Then the image opens of the two relevant opens
of `Spec R_g` are `ι ''ᵁ ⊤ = D(g)` and `ι ''ᵁ D(f̄) = D(gf)` (with `f̄ = algebraMap R R_g f`), where
the images are taken in the iterated-restriction form `(specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((iso).inv ''ᵁ -)`
matching `modulesRestrictBasicOpen`.  Project-local: the opens bookkeeping that lets the
`R_g`-section localization on the tile be matched against the `R`-section restriction
`Γ(D(g),F) → Γ(D(gf),F)`. -/
lemma tile_image_opens_identities (g f : R) :
    (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
        (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)) = specBasicOpen g ∧
      (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
        (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f))) = specBasicOpen (g * f) := by
  constructor
  · rw [show ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
        (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)) = ⊤ from by
          rw [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]; exact Scheme.Hom.opensRange_of_isIso _]
    simp [Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange]
  · rw [← Scheme.Hom.image_comp]
    show specAwayToSpec g ''ᵁ _ = _
    rw [specAwayToSpec_eq]
    apply Opens.ext
    rw [Scheme.Hom.coe_image]
    rw [show ⇑(Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))))
        = PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)) from rfl]
    ext x
    simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, Set.mem_image]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨p, hp, rfl⟩
      have hpr : p.asIdeal.IsPrime := p.isPrime
      refine ⟨?_, hp⟩
      change (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g)) g ∉ p.asIdeal
      intro hmem
      exact hpr.ne_top (Ideal.eq_top_of_isUnit_mem _ hmem
        (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit g))
    · rintro ⟨hg, hf⟩
      have hx : x ∈ Set.range (PrimeSpectrum.comap (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g))) := by
        rw [PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range (Localization.Away g) g]; exact hg
      obtain ⟨p, rfl⟩ := hx
      exact ⟨p, hf, rfl⟩

end TileSectionLocalization

open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry TopologicalSpace Opposite

variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

    (P : (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F).Presentation) :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (show specBasicOpen (g * f) ≤ specBasicOpen g from by
          rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul]; exact inf_le_left)).op).hom := by
  set fbar : Localization.Away g := algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f with hfbar
  have hrho' := section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F) P fbar
  have hdesc := isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap (R := R)
    (A := Localization.Away g) (f := f) _ hrho'
  obtain ⟨hW0eq, hW1eq⟩ := tile_image_opens_identities (R := R) g f
  set W0 := (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
    (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)) with hW0
  set W1 := (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f))) with hW1
  have hW : W1 ≤ W0 := by
    rw [hW0, hW1]; apply Scheme.Hom.image_mono; apply Scheme.Hom.image_mono; exact le_top
  -- hdesc as F-side map W1 ≤ W0

      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE hW).op).hom := hdesc
  -- the linear equivs from eqToIso
  let esrc : (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (op (specBasicOpen g)) ≃ₗ[R]
      (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (op W0) :=
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (by rw [hW0eq])).op).toLinearEquiv
  let ecod : (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (op W1) ≃ₗ[R]
      (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (op (specBasicOpen (g * f))) :=
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (by rw [hW1eq])).op).toLinearEquiv

    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE hW).op).hom esrc (hf := hdesc2)

    (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE hW).op).hom ∘ₗ esrc.toLinearMap) ecod
    (hf := h1)

/-- **Per-tile section localization at `f` (Stacks 01HV(4)/01I8) — the last keystone leaf.**  Let `F`
be an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module, `f g : R`, and suppose the tile `F_{(g)} = modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F`
over `Spec R_g` admits a global presentation `P` (this holds when `D(g)` lies in a presentation
member of a quasi-coherence cover, via `presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen` (B4)).  Then the
section-restriction `Γ(D(g), F) → Γ(D(gf), F)` exhibits its target as the localization of its source
at the powers of `f`.

The proof is the honest base-ring descent of the blueprint, but it turns out Sub-lemma B (the
`R_g`-section ↔ `R`-section comparison, `lem:tile_section_comparison`) is *definitional*: the carriers
`Γ_{R_g}(V, F_{(g)})` and `Γ_R((D(g) ↪ X) ''ᵁ ((Spec R_g ≅ D(g)) ''ᵁ V), F)` are defeq (iterated
`restrict_obj`), and the scalar coherence `algebraMap R R_g r • x = r • x` across the two module
structures is `rfl`.  Hence: (Step 2) `section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation` localizes the tile
over `R_g` at `algebraMap f`; (Step 5) `isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap`
descends the base ring to `R` — landing, by the definitional comparison, on the `R`-section
restriction between the image opens `W₀, W₁`; (Step 3) `tile_image_opens_identities` rewrites
`W₀ = D(g)`, `W₁ = D(gf)` to match the target.  Project-local: the per-tile localization datum the
sheaf-axiom kernel comparison (`qcoh_section_kernel_comparison`) consumes; non-circular because the
localization happens entirely on the tile (where `F` is `tilde`-of-a-module), never on global
`Γ(X, F)`. -/
lemma tile_section_localization (F : (Spec R).Modules) (g f : R)
    (P : (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F).Presentation) :
    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (show specBasicOpen (g * f) ≤ specBasicOpen g from by
          rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul]; exact inf_le_left)).op).hom := by
  -- Step 2: localize the globally-presented tile over `R_g` at `f̄ = algebraMap f`.
  have hrho' := section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F) P
    (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f)
  -- Step 5: descend the base ring `R_g → R`.  By the definitional section comparison (Sub-lemma B),
  -- this lands on the `R`-section restriction between the image opens `W₁ ≤ W₀`.
  have hdesc := isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap (R := R)
    (A := Localization.Away g) (f := f) _ hrho'
  -- Step 3: the image opens of the affine identification (Sub-lemma A).
  obtain ⟨hW0eq, hW1eq⟩ := tile_image_opens_identities (R := R) g f
  have hW : (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
      (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (algebraMap R (Localization.Away g) f)))
      ≤ (specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ
      (⊤ : (Spec (.of (Localization.Away g))).Opens)) := by
    apply Scheme.Hom.image_mono; apply Scheme.Hom.image_mono; exact le_top
  have hdesc2 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE hW).op).hom := hdesc
  rw [hW0eq, hW1eq] at hdesc2
  exact hdesc2

end TileSectionLocalization

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/QcohTildeSections.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (axiom-clean, 1):** `tile_image_opens_identities` (Sub-lemma A) — lines ~725–767.

  (`tile_section_comparison`), which is **genuinely non-definitional** (confirmed via clean
  `lake env lean`). Not added (no sorry left); a precise deferred-note comment is in the file.
- **Import added:** `import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.QcohRestrictBasicOpen` (needed for the tile
  infrastructure `modulesRestrictBasicOpen`, `specBasicOpen`, `specAwayToSpec`, `specAwayToSpec_eq`,
  `basicOpenIsoSpecAway`, `presentationModulesRestrictBasicOpen`). No import cycle.
- **sorry count:** 0 → 0 (file remains sorry-free; `lake env lean` EXIT 0; olean rebuilt OK).

## tile_image_opens_identities (Sub-lemma A) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** for `g f : R`, the two image opens of the affine identification
  `ι = specAwayToSpec g`:
  - `(specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ ⊤) = specBasicOpen g`
  - `(specBasicOpen g).ι ''ᵁ ((basicOpenIsoSpecAway g).inv ''ᵁ (basicOpen (algebraMap R R_g f)))
     = specBasicOpen (g * f)`
- **Approach:** identity 1 via `Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange` + `opensRange_of_isIso`.
  Identity 2 via `Scheme.Hom.comp_image` (collapse the two `''ᵁ` to `specAwayToSpec g ''ᵁ -`),
  `specAwayToSpec_eq` (the map is `Spec.map (algebraMap R R_g)`), then a membership computation:
  `comap '' basicOpen(algebraMap f) = basicOpen f ∩ range comap = basicOpen f ∩ basicOpen g
  = basicOpen (g*f)`, using `PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range`, `comap_basicOpen`,
  `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`, `basicOpen_mul`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED. `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## tile_section_localization — NOT ADDED (Sub-lemma B is the blocker)
- **Goal:** `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)` of the `R`-section restriction
  `Γ(D(g),F) → Γ(D(gf),F)` for a tile with a global presentation `P`.
- **Verified-correct assembly skeleton** (all pieces present in this file):
  Step 2 `section_isLocalizedModule_of_presentation (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F) P (algebraMap f)`
  → `IsLocalizedModule (powers (algebraMap f))` over `R_g`; then a base-ring descent via
  `isLocalizedModule_powers_restrictScalars_of_algebraMap`; then `tile_image_opens_identities`
  to rewrite the image opens to `D(g)`, `D(gf)`.
- **THE BLOCKER — Sub-lemma B is non-definitional (iter-041 finding CONFIRMED on a clean build).**
  I initially found (via `lean_run_code`) that the tile section carriers were defeq to the `F`-side
  sections and that the scalar coherence `algebraMap R R_g r • x = r • x` was `rfl` — which would
  have made `tile_section_localization` collapse to a few lines. **These run_code successes were
  artifacts of a stale `.olean`.** A clean `lake env lean` on a minimal test
  (`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CoherenceTest.lean`, since removed) gives a hard **type mismatch**:
  - `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (modulesRestrictBasicOpen g F)).presheaf.obj (op V) : ModuleCat ↑(Localization.Away g)`
  - `(modulesSpecToSheaf.obj F).presheaf.obj (op (ι ''ᵁ V))                    : ModuleCat ↑R`

    are **not the same type** (different base rings — `modulesSpecToSheaf` over `R_g` restricts
    scalars to `R_g`, over `R` to `R`). So neither the carriers nor the scalar coherence are `rfl`.
  This vindicates project memory `[[keystone-tile-reconciliation-not-rfl]]` exactly.
- **What Sub-lemma B genuinely needs (next iter):** the honest natural `R_g`-linear isomorphism
  `Γ_{R_g}(V, F_{(g)}) ≅ Γ_R(ι ''ᵁ V, F)` intertwining restriction maps, built from the structure-
  sheaf bookkeeping of `modulesSpecToSheaf` (`StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso` for `R` and `R_g`,
  the restrict-functor scalar comparison, and the `restrict_obj`/`restrict_map` defeqs which are rfl
  ONLY at the local-ring `SheafOfModules` `Γ(M,-)` level, NOT the global-ring `modulesSpecToSheaf`
  level). ~100–150 LOC. Once it lands, the descent assembles exactly as the skeleton above.
- **DEAD-END WARNINGS (do not repeat):**
  1. Do NOT trust `lean_run_code`/LSP `rfl`/instance successes for these tile defeqs — the imported
     `.olean` can be stale and report defeqs/instances that a real `lake env lean` build rejects.
     Always confirm tile-section defeq claims with `lake env lean` on a fresh test file.
  2. The naive "carriers defeq + scalar coherence rfl ⟹ descent is trivial" route is UNSOUND
     (carriers are not even the same type). This is the same trap iter-041 flagged.
  3. `inferInstanceAs (Module R (F-side))` assigned to the `R_g`-tile type does not typecheck in a
     real build (different ring types), and even where it elaborates it triggers
     `dependsOnNoncomputable` on the in-proof `letI` and an unresolvable scalar-coherence `rfl`.

## Needs blueprint entry
- **`tile_image_opens_identities`** (NEW non-private lemma, this file). Blueprint label
  `lem:tile_image_opens_identities` already exists (currently with deferred `\lean{}` pin per the
  blueprint NOTE). **Action for planner/reviewer:** pin
  `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tile_image_opens_identities}` to that block and mark it for `\leanok`.
  Proof relies on: `Scheme.Hom.image_top_eq_opensRange`, `Scheme.Hom.opensRange_of_isIso`,
  `Scheme.Hom.comp_image`, `specAwayToSpec_eq` (QcohRestrictBasicOpen),
  `PrimeSpectrum.localization_away_comap_range`, `PrimeSpectrum.comap_basicOpen`,
  `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul`, `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`.
- `lem:tile_section_comparison` (Sub-lemma B) and `lem:tile_section_localization` remain unformalized
  (no `\leanok`); the blueprint sketch for `lem:tile_section_comparison` (honest 5-step descent) is
  correct and should be kept — it accurately describes the genuine ~100–150 LOC construction.

## Why I stopped

  (`archon-informal-agent.py` unavailable); used LSP + lake throughout.
